Human.description: Humans are the most adaptable and ambitious people among the common races. Whatever drives them, humans are the innovators, the achievers, and the pioneers.

traits.name: Age
traits.description: Humans reach adulthood in their late teens and live less than a century.

traits.name: Size
traits.description: Humans vary widely in height and build, from barely 5 feet to well over 6 feet tall. Your size is Medium.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

Elf.description: Elves are a magical people of otherworldly grace, living in the world but not entirely part of it.

High Elf.description: As a high elf, you have a keen mind and a mastery of at least the basics of magic.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: Cantrip
traits.description: You know one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

Wood Elf.description: As a wood elf, you have keen senses and deep intuition, and your fleet feet carry you quickly through your native forests.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

traits.name: Mask of the Wild
traits.description: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

Dark Elf (Drow).description: Descended from an earlier subrace of elves, the drow were banished from the surface world for following the goddess Lolth down the path to evil.

traits.name: Superior Darkvision
traits.description: Your darkvision has a radius of 120 feet.

traits.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
traits.description: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

traits.name: Drow Magic
traits.description: You know the dancing lights cantrip. At 3rd level, you can cast faerie fire once per day. At 5th level, you can cast darkness once per day. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

traits.name: Drow Weapon Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with rapiers, shortswords, and hand crossbows.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.description: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply for 4 hours a day.

Dwarf.description: Bold and hardy, dwarves are known as skilled warriors, miners, and workers of stone and metal.

Mountain Dwarf.description: As a mountain dwarf, you're strong and hardy, accustomed to a difficult life in rugged terrain.

traits.name: Dwarven Armor Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with light and medium armor.

Hill Dwarf.description: As a hill dwarf, you have keen senses, deep intuition, and remarkable resilience.

traits.name: Dwarven Toughness
traits.description: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and it increases by 1 every time you gain a level.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Dwarven Combat Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient and add double your proficiency bonus.

Halfling.description: The diminutive halflings survive in a world full of larger creatures by avoiding notice or, barring that, avoiding offense.

Lightfoot Halfling.description: As a lightfoot halfling, you can easily hide from notice, even using other people as cover.

traits.name: Naturally Stealthy
traits.description: You can attempt to hide even when you are obscured only by a creature that is at least one size larger than you.

Stout Halfling.description: As a stout halfling, you're hardier than average and have some resistance to poison.

traits.name: Stout Resilience
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.description: When you roll a 1 on an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

traits.name: Brave
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.description: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

Dragonborn.description: Born of dragons, as their name proclaims, the dragonborn walk proudly through a world that greets them with fearful incomprehension.

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.description: You have draconic ancestry. Choose one type of dragon from the Draconic Ancestry table. Your breath weapon and damage resistance are determined by the dragon type.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.description: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation. When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area must make a saving throw (DC = 8 + CON mod + proficiency). A creature takes 2d6 damage on a failed save, and half as much on a successful one. Damage increases to 3d6 at 6th level, 4d6 at 11th, and 5d6 at 16th. Can use once per short/long rest.

traits.name: Damage Resistance
traits.description: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

Gnome.description: A gnome's energy and enthusiasm for living shines through every inch of his or her tiny body.

Forest Gnome.description: As a forest gnome, you have a natural knack for illusion and inherent quickness and stealth.

traits.name: Natural Illusionist
traits.description: You know the minor illusion cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

traits.name: Speak with Small Beasts
traits.description: Through sounds and gestures, you can communicate simple ideas with Small or smaller beasts.

Rock Gnome.description: As a rock gnome, you have a natural inventiveness and hardiness beyond that of other gnomes.

traits.name: Artificer's Lore
traits.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to magic items, alchemical objects, or technological devices, you can add twice your proficiency bonus.

traits.name: Tinker
traits.description: You have proficiency with artisan's tools (tinker's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp).

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Gnome Cunning
traits.description: You have advantage on all Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma saving throws against magic.

Half-Elf.description: Walking in two worlds but truly belonging to neither, half-elves combine what some say are the best qualities of both races.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Skill Versatility
traits.description: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

Half-Orc.description: Whether united under the leadership of a mighty warlock or fighting for survival, half-orcs and orcs survive by virtue of their sheer toughness.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Menacing
traits.description: You gain proficiency in the Intimidation skill.

traits.name: Relentless Endurance
traits.description: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

traits.name: Savage Attacks
traits.description: When you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice one additional time and add it to the extra damage of the critical hit.

Tiefling.description: To be greeted with stares and whispers, to suffer violence and insult on the street, to see mistrust and fear in every eye: this is the lot of the tiefling.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Hellish Resistance
traits.description: You have resistance to fire damage.

traits.name: Infernal Legacy
traits.description: You know the thaumaturgy cantrip. When you reach 3rd level, you can cast the hellish rebuke spell once per day. When you reach 5th level, you can cast the darkness spell once per day. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.
